claims.statement: PowerShell event log shows a PowerShell command was executed at 2026-01-14T10:49:44.573257Z with command: 'powershell $logs = Get-WinEvent -ListLog * | Where-Object {$_.RecordCount} | Select-Object -ExpandProperty LogName ; ForEach ( $l in $logs | Sort | Get-Unique ) {[System.Diagnostics.Eventing.Reader.EventLogSession]::GlobalSession.ClearLog($l)}'

The PowerShell command found in Windows PowerShell.evtx is a script designed to clear all Windows event logs that have record counts, matching the log clearing events observed in Security.evtx and System.evtx
User jasonr executed a PowerShell script to clear all Windows event logs at 10:49:44Z, which is a common anti-forensics technique to cover tracks
Multiple external SMB logons from IP 195.101.67.3 (source computer DCFS) occurred starting at 11:41:03Z, approximately 52 minutes after the log clearing activity
The log clearing at 10:49:44Z may have been preparatory activity before external attack attempts from IP 195.101.67.3 starting at 11:41:03Z

next_steps: Investigate if there were any successful logons after the external SMB attempts
Examine other event logs for additional suspicious activity around the same timeframe
Check for any file creation or modification events around the log clearing time
